title: Vehicle
description:
A complete vehicle description.

`extends` and `extensions` are the two contract-level affordances: inheritance is a single
`extends:` parent chain (resolved to `None` in the loaded model), and `x-*` vendor keys are
gathered into `extensions`. Whole optional subsystems (`ers`, `battery`) are `Option`; see the
crate docs for the required-vs-optional rule.

aero.description: Aerodynamics (map reference + optional constant degenerate coefficients).

battery.description: Battery equivalent-circuit model reference, if the car has one.

driver.description:
Ideal-driver preview/tracking gains for the transient tiers (defaulted; MacAdam preview + PI
speed tracking, §7.7). Absent ⇒ literature defaults, surfaced as estimated.

drivetrain.description: Drivetrain topology graph (sources → couplers → wheels) plus the control layer.

ers.description: Energy-recovery system (MGU-K + energy store), if the car has one.

extends.description: Optional single preset this document inherits from (resolved away in the loaded model).

fuel.description:
On-board fuel (mass, CG offset, flow limit), if the car burns fuel. Absent ⇒ mass is the
all-inclusive `chassis.mass_kg` and results reproduce v0.3.0 byte-identically (§8.1, D-M6-4).

oneOf.description: Activated by the energy-management strategy layer (default).
Always available where the regulations permit.

Aero.description:
Aerodynamics block.

The primary representation is a gridded map (sidecar parquet/CSV referenced by `map`) whose
axes are named in `axes` (e.g. `["ride_height_f_mm", "ride_height_r_mm", "yaw_deg"]`).
A passenger car degenerates to constant `CdA`/`ClA`, given via `constant`.

axes.description: Ordered names of the map's input axes.

constant.description: Optional constant coefficients (the passenger-car degenerate case, §7.4).

map.description: Reference to the gridded aero map (parquet/CSV sidecar).

AeroConstant.description: Constant aerodynamic coefficients × frontal area (the degenerate, non-mapped case).

AxleKc.description:
Per-axle kinematics & compliance.

`static_ride_height_m`, `anti_dive`, `anti_squat`, `camber_map`, and `toe_map` are **estimable**:
when omitted, the estimation stage fills them from documented heuristics (ride height →
axle-nominal, kinematic angles → 0, maps → identity) and records a line in the loaded-model
report.

ride_rate_n_per_m.description: Vertical ride rate at the wheel, N/m.

roll_stiffness_share.description: Fraction of total roll stiffness carried by this axle, 0..1.

static_ride_height_m.description:
Static (design) ride height at the wheel with the car at rest, m (estimable → axle-nominal).

The reference platform the T1 aero-platform equilibrium (§7.4) compresses under downforce:
`h = static − ΔF_spring / (2·ride_rate)`. Only consumed by the ride-height aero map; the
constant-aero degenerate path ignores it.

Battery.description: Battery block: an equivalent-circuit model selector plus its parameter file.

params.description: Reference to the parameter file (Thevenin RC-pair parameters).

oneOf.description: Thevenin equivalent circuit with one or more RC pairs.

BatteryRef.description: Reference to a battery equivalent-circuit parameter file.

BrakeDisc.description: A single brake disc's thermal properties.

regen_blend.description: Optional regen/friction blending (present when an ERS/battery can recover braking energy).

Chassis.description:
Chassis bulk properties in the ISO 8855 body frame (x forward, y left, z up).

`inertia` is a diagonal `[Ixx, Iyy, Izz]` for v1 (7/14-DOF tiers); products of inertia are an
additive MINOR field later.

cg.description: Centre of gravity `[x, y, z]`, m, in the body frame.

inertia.description: Diagonal moments of inertia `[Ixx, Iyy, Izz]`, kg·m².

Coupler.description:
A coupler element on a drive path.

Externally tagged (serde default), so the wire forms are `{gearbox: {...}}`, `{diff: {...}}`,
and `{fixed_ratio: 2.4}`. A standalone clutch coupler is deferred; shift/clutch dynamics live
inside [`Gearbox`].

oneOf.description: A multi-ratio gearbox with a final drive.

per_lap_deploy_mj.description:
Optional per-lap deployment budget, MJ (electrical). Generic config for non-F1 rule sets —
the 2026 F1 regulations impose NO per-lap deployment budget (deployment is bounded only by
the power curves and the SoC window); leave it absent for a 2026 car. Never estimated.

power_limit_kw.description:
Peak deployment power, kW — ELECTRICAL DC power at the CU-K bus (FIA 2026 C5.2.7), not
mechanical crank power.

taper_vs_speed.description:
Power-vs-speed taper (evaluated piecewise-linearly — the regulatory closed-form curves of
C5.2.8 are the breakpoints, not samples of a smooth map).

Diff.description:
A differential.

`preload_nm` is **conditionally required**: the semantic stage requires it for
[`DiffKind::Lsd`] and [`DiffKind::Locked`]. `ramp` (`[accel, decel]`) applies to LSDs only.

type.description: The differential kind. On the wire this is the key `type`.

oneOf.description: Solid axle (locked-diff limit case; day-1 support for karts/live axles).

DriveControl.description: The rule-based control layer: static splits + torque vectoring.

DriveUnit.description: A single torque source and the ordered coupler path from it to the wheels it drives.

path.description: Ordered couplers from the source shaft toward the wheels.

source.description: The `.ptm` map for this source (ICE, electric machine, or lumped drive unit).

thermal.description: Optional `.emotor` thermal model — electric machines only (§9.5).

wheels.description: The wheels this unit ultimately drives.

Driver.description:
Ideal-driver control gains: MacAdam-style preview steering + PI speed tracking (§7.7).

Optional throughout; unset gains take the `DEFAULT_*` literature values (tuned on
`limebeer_2014_f1`) and are reported as estimated. Numbers are SI (rad, m, s); pedal gains are
dimensionless-pedal `[0, 1]` per error unit.

ff_accel_scale_mps2.description:
Feed-forward normalising acceleration — the friction-circle radius (the gg-headroom usable
accel that maps a demanded longitudinal acceleration to a `[−1, 1]` pedal command), m/s².

preview_time_s.description: MacAdam preview time, s (look-ahead distance `L_p = preview_time · v`).

sideslip_damping.description:
Sideslip-damping steer gain `k_β`, rad/rad — steers the heading back toward the velocity
vector (`δ −= k_β·β`). This is the correction for a *translational* slide (the car crabbing
with near-zero yaw rate), which the yaw-rate damper cannot see.

stability_slip_gain.description: Throttle-cut rate per rad of sideslip past the limit, 1/rad (a lift-when-loose stability aid).

stability_slip_limit_rad.description: Sideslip magnitude at which the stability throttle-cut begins, rad.

traction_slip_gain.description: Pedal-governor cut rate per unit of slip ratio past the limit.

traction_slip_limit.description:
Drive-wheel slip ratio at which the driver's pedal governor starts cutting throttle (the
slip near the tire's force peak). With race gearing, low-gear torque is a multiple of the
grip limit; an ideal driver modulates the pedal against wheelspin.

Drivetrain.description: The drivetrain: one or more drive units plus the control layer.

shift_maps.description:
Optional named up-shift maps selectable per station by a `u(s)` `shift_map_id` schedule
(§8.3, D-M6-9). The DERIVED schedule (from the gear force curves) is the implicit default
map (id 0); a `shift_maps` entry with `name == "default"` overrides that default. Absent ⇒
only the derived default exists ⇒ byte-identical to pre-1.8.

units.description: Torque sources and the coupler paths from each to its wheels.

Efficiency.description: Drivetrain efficiency: a single constant or a gridded map reference.

anyOf.description: A gridded efficiency map (parquet/CSV sidecar), e.g. `{map: eff.parquet}`.

EmotorRef.description: Reference to an `.emotor` electric-machine thermal file.

EnergyStore.description: Energy-store sizing and usable state-of-charge window.

capacity_mj.description:
Usable-window energy, MJ — the energy spanned by `soc_window` on the pack, not the pack's
total capacity (the FIA 2026 C5.2.9 limit is a max−min SoC *window* of 4 MJ on track; the
regulations set no total capacity).

soc_window.description: Usable SOC window `[min, max]`, each in 0..1, ascending.

elec_mech_factor.description:
Fixed electrical→mechanical conversion factor between the CU-K DC bus (where the
regulatory power caps and energy ledgers live) and the crank (FIA 2026 C5.2.14; the
harvest direction uses its inverse per C5.2.21). Default 0.97.

es.description: Energy store sizing/window (battery physics lives in the battery block).

Extensions.description:
The `x-*` extension bag: vendor/experimental keys that are carried through but not interpreted.

Only keys beginning `x-` may appear here; the unknown-key walk (stage 4) routes `x-*` keys into
this bag with a warning and rejects any other unknown key as a hard error (§9 versioning rule).

Fuel.description:
On-board fuel: initial race load, tank capacity, optional CG offset, LHV, and flow limit.

The tank centroid CG offset is expressed relative to the DRY-mass (empty-tank) centre of
gravity in the ISO 8855 body frame (x forward, z up). The full-tank CG is the mass-weighted
blend of the dry CG and the tank centroid; as fuel burns the CG migrates linearly back toward
the dry CG (D-M6-4d).

cg_offset_m.description:
Fuel-tank centroid `[x, z]` offset (m) from the DRY-mass CG, ISO 8855 (+x forward, +z up).
Absent ⇒ CG does not migrate (mass-only feedback).

flow_limit.description:
Optional fuel-flow limit (F1-style, §8.1 / FIA C5.2.3–C5.2.5). Energy-only form (D-M6-5):
a constraint on available ICE power, never a clamp on the ṁ accounting.

initial_kg.description:
Initial (race-start) fuel mass, kg. Typical F1 race load 70–80 kg. ADDS on top of the
all-inclusive `chassis.mass_kg`: `m₀ = chassis.mass_kg + initial_kg`.

lhv_j_per_kg.description:
Lower heating value, J/kg (defaults to 43.0e6). The config home of the fuel↔energy
conversion; ṁ_fuel = P_chem / lhv.

tank_kg.description: Tank capacity, kg (`initial_kg ≤ tank_kg`). Fuel mass is clamped to `[0, tank_kg]`.

FuelFlowLimit.description:
The fuel-flow limit as an ENERGY constraint (MJ/h). The kg/h ↔ MJ/h equivalence goes through
[`Fuel::lhv_j_per_kg`]; §8.1's "ṁ_max" is recorded as satisfied-by-energy-equivalence.

mj_per_h.description: Absolute energy-flow cap, MJ/h (the FIA C5.2.4 100 kg/h ≈ 4300 MJ/h at 43 MJ/kg).

rpm_line.description: Optional low-rpm line (FIA C5.2.5): below `below_rpm`, `EF(MJ/h) = slope·N + intercept`.

Gearbox.description: A gearbox: ordered ratios, final drive, shift time, and efficiency.

efficiency.description: Mechanical efficiency (constant or map). Defaults to a constant 0.985.

MapRef.description: Reference to a numeric sidecar map table (parquet/CSV), e.g. an aero or K&C map.

OverrideMode.description:
Manual override mode: a separate deployment envelope with extra per-lap energy.

A distinct type (not [`Deployment`]) because it carries `extra_energy_per_lap_mj` and an
`activation` policy that base deployment lacks.

extra_energy_per_lap_mj.description:
Extra per-lap HARVEST allowance while override is active, MJ (estimable; defaults to 0).
FIA 2026 C5.2.10iii: the +0.5 MJ granted with Override is additional *Recharge* (harvest)
allowance — it is NOT a deployment budget.

taper_vs_speed.description: Override power-vs-speed taper (typically a higher-speed taper than base deployment).

PresetRef.description: Reference to a preset the document `extends:`, e.g. `presets/formula_base`.

PtmRef.description: Reference to a `.ptm` neutral powertrain-map file.

braking_power_limit_kw.description:
Peak braking-recovery power, kW — ELECTRICAL DC power at the CU-K bus (FIA 2026 C5.2.7
caps both directions at the same bus).

per_lap_harvest_mj.description:
Per-lap harvest ("Recharge") budget, MJ (electrical; FIA 2026 C5.2.10). ALL harvest paths
— braking, part-throttle, ICE-driven — count against this one budget.

ramp_initial_step_kw.description:
Recharge-phase ramp: maximum initial power-demand step, kW (FIA 2026 C5.12.4 "power
limited" ramp-down, simplified). Default 150.

ramp_rate_kw_per_s.description:
Recharge-phase ramp: maximum demand-reduction rate after the initial step, kW per second
(C5.12.5, the conservative bound of the reg's 50–100 range). Default 50.

ramp_total_kw.description: Recharge-phase ramp: maximum total demand reduction, kW (C5.12.6). Default 700.

recharge_phases.description:
Whether dedicated recharge phases (part-throttle harvest and full-throttle ICE-driven
back-drive on straights) are modelled.

recharge_target_soc.description:
Target pack SoC the automated Recharge paths steer toward (the 2026 ECU's selectable
"Recharge target"). Must lie inside `es.soc_window`. Default: mid-window.

RegenBlend.description: Regen/friction blending policy for recovered braking.

front_bias.description: Optional front-axle regen bias fraction, 0..1 (defaults to the friction balance if omitted).

max_regen_frac.description:
Maximum fraction of the **driven axle's** brake torque provided by regen, 0..1 (the machine can
only ever brake its own axle). The calipers supply the deficit, so the axle's total brake torque
— and the trajectory — is unchanged: this is series/blended braking, not added deceleration.

RpmFlowLine.description:
The FIA C5.2.5 low-rpm fuel-flow line: `EF(MJ/h) = slope·N + intercept` for `N < below_rpm`.
F1 2026 values are `below_rpm = 10500`, `slope = 0.27`, `intercept = 165`.

below_rpm.description: Crank speed (rpm) below which the linear line applies (at/above it, the flat `mj_per_h`).

slope_mj_per_h_per_rpm.description: Slope of the line, MJ/h per rpm.

SchemaVersion.description: Schema version of the form <name>/<MAJOR>.<MINOR>, e.g. vehicle/1.0

ShiftMap.description:
A named up-shift map: either explicit per-gear crossover speeds or a scalar factor on the
derived schedule (D-M6-9). Selected per station by a `u(s)` `shift_map_id` schedule.

name.description: Map name — must be unique across `shift_maps`. `"default"` overrides the derived default.

oneOf.description:
Explicit per-gear up-shift crossover speeds, m/s (length must equal the up-shift count, i.e.
one fewer than the gear count). Index 0 = the 1→2 up-shift speed.

A single positive multiplier applied to every derived up-shift speed (`> 1` shifts later,
`< 1` shifts earlier). A factor of exactly `1.0` reproduces the derived default.

SpeedTaper.description: A power-vs-speed taper table (paired equal-length arrays; monotone non-increasing power).

Split.description: Static torque splits. `front` is the front-axle share; `left` is the left-side share.

front.description: Front-axle torque share, 0..1 (omit for single-axle cars).

left.description: Left-side torque share, 0..1 (omit unless per-side allocation applies).

Suspension.description: Suspension block: a model selector plus front/rear axle K&C.

Tires.description: Front/rear tire references. Both are required (load-bearing, no sane default).

TorqueVectoring.description:
Yaw-moment-proportional torque vectoring: `ΔM_z = k_yaw · (r_target − r)`, with the demanded
moment physically allocated across the driven wheels within their friction-ellipse and
machine-envelope limits (HANDOFF §8.0; the allocator interface is shaped so a QP replaces the
rule-based split post-v1, Decision #2).

max_yaw_moment_nm.description:
Optional hard cap on the commanded yaw moment `|ΔM_z|`, N·m (a machine-envelope proxy). When
omitted, the friction-ellipse per-wheel limits alone bound the allocation.
